item.title: Re: Author's Post
item.content:encoded: Tiny detail, but the last paragraph about the researcher requesting amnestics and a transfer feels like it ends abruptly. Maybe add a short sentence after the last one saying if the request was granted?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Simple, interesting, and reminiscent of series 1, which I am a fan of personally. Others may need a pervasive backstory to feel more drawn in, but I see the value in the simply anomalous. +1 for weird music thingy.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
i really like it

Keter murzbow album when

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Most of this didn't do much for me, but

Soon, I'll either climb up towards Hell or tunnel into stillbirth again.

just pushes it into +1 territory.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It’s very much a type of style that people love or hate, which is a thing I aimed for in writing this. I apologize for nothing, but nonetheless, thank you for reading and leaving critique. 💜

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I'm not sure if this works, honestly.

There isn't a lot here beyond the actual effect, and I'm not particularly moved by the spoken-word portion, mostly due to my own relative disdain for 'Musician expounding their truth' trope in music.

There's nothing I can point to that's wrong with the article, so much as it didn't grab me much, and actively turned me off of the work during the spoken word section. I feel like this would appeal to people who are in to spoken word self-exposition; that's just not me.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Cryptic stuff can be a hard sell, but I think you pulled it off nicely here. All the lyrics felt like they had a deeper meaning to them that I’d be able to find if I looked hard enough.

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This is no different than the normal Life in a Peaceful New World downvoted

It's short enough to work for me and it makes me want to listen to more noise music again, so this gets a +1 from me

item.title: Author's Post
item.content:encoded:
not a doomscon article

part one of three completely unrelated articles about people using music as a means of catharsis

for the record, experimental records of this nature that have these kind of poetic/cryptic/over the top lyrics or spoken word go a lot hammier and serious and abstract than i did here. i toned the purple way down if anything, but it still feels weird and has internal meaning.

Taffeta gave me clinical tone critique and grammar critique because that is my biggest weakness when i make skips
